summary: Parallel review: FAIL (3 critical, 3 warning, 4 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: The handshake claim is verified: `roles/simplicity.md:18-24` defines all four veto categories (dead code, premature abstraction, unnecessary indirection, gold-plating) under "Veto Authority (🔴 Required)", and line 26 reserves 🟡 for non-veto complexity. The file follows the existing `roles/*.md` pattern — no new modules, dependencies, or boundary changes from an architect's lens. Gate output shows tests passing.

Verified `roles/simplicity.md:18-26` defines all four veto categories (dead code, premature abstraction, unnecessary indirection, gold-plating) with concrete operational sub-criteria and explicitly reserves 🟡 for out-of-scope complexity concerns. The diff also correctly removed the now-contradictory "Don't block on speculative complexity" anti-pattern. Gate output shows the visible test suites passing.

Spec match confirmed: `roles/simplicity.md:21-24` defines the four veto categories verbatim (dead code, premature abstraction, unnecessary indirection, gold-plating); lines 18–19 require 🔴 within them and line 26 reserves 🟡 for everything else. The `test/synthesize-compound.test.mjs` companion edit is in-scope plumbing tied to the unwired fabricated-refs layer (commits 40d4502 / 74d71f1), not scope creep. No critical or warning findings. Full evaluation appended to `.team/features/simplicity-reviewer-with-veto/tasks/task-1/eval.md`.
- `roles/simplicity.md:18–26` lists the four veto categories and ties 🔴/🟡 correctly.

🟡 bin/lib/compound-gate.mjs:189 — Commented `detectFabricatedRefs` call leaves the runCompoundGate integration uncovered; add a negative test asserting `runCompoundGate(fabricatedFindings, dir).layers` does NOT include `"fabricated-refs"` today, so flipping the comment forces a deliberate test update.

Eval appended to `.team/features/simplicity-reviewer-with-veto/tasks/task-1/eval.md`. Verified via `npm test` (541/541 pass) and direct read of `roles/simplicity.md` (32 lines, four veto categories at lines 18–26, 🟡 reserved for non-veto at line 26). PR adds no new abstractions, indirection, dead code, or gold-plating; test changes reduce indirection rather than add it.
